studies.officialTitle: A Study of Multiple Doses of NNC0487-0111 in People With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Adults with overweight or obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: All treatment-emergent adverse events were mild or moderate; gastrointestinal symptoms and decreased appetite were most common and dose related. In Part C/D (12-week dosing), serious adverse events occurred in 0% (50 mg), 0% (2x50 mg), and 6% (2x25 mg) vs. 0% placebo; nausea in 31%, 75%, and 94% vs. 8%; vomiting in 38%, 56%, and 63% vs. 0%. Across all parts (A-D), 8 of 111 (7%) participants on active treatment were anti-drug-antibody positive; no per-arm breakdown for Part C/D was reported. No deaths occurred.

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Subcutaneous Amycretin in People With Overweight or Obesity
studies.population: Adults aged 18-55 years with overweight or obesity.
studies.safetySummary: A high frequency of gastrointestinal events was reported, consistent with GLP-1 and amylin agonism; no unexpected safety signal was disclosed. Serious adverse events occurred in 0-3% of amycretin arms vs. 0% placebo across parts B-E. Nausea (50-82% vs. 25-60%) and vomiting (25-53% vs. 0-60%) increased with dose. Anti-drug antibodies (low titres) occurred in 29% (Part B, 60 mg), 21% (Part C, 20 mg), and 38% (Part D, 5 mg) of active-arm participants and did not affect safety or pharmacodynamics; not reported for Part E or placebo. No deaths occurred; one case of recurrent gallstone pancreatitis was reported.

studies.officialTitle: Efficacy and Safety of NNC0487-0111 s.c. Once Weekly in Participants With Obesity Who Have Reached Target Dose During run-in Period (AMAZE 12)
studies.population: Participants with obesity who reached target dose during run-in.
